fields.description:
## Context / Problem

`docs/llm-framework.md` documents the four-pass contract and says projects may add code-review
criteria through the same overlay as plan-review, but it does not document the runtime contract for
how a project code-review criterion flows through the pipeline once the seam is complete — the
contract the epic's seam children (fan-in, Pass-3 routing, eval/CLI admission) deliver. Without a
written contract the behavior is undocumented and can silently regress. This is the last item in
the stack: a purely mechanical documentation change with no new testable behavior. It **depends on**
the CLI-admission child `[rebar:41e2-a0a2-2db4-4847]`, the surface-completing seam ticket, and
documents the end-state contract that ticket (and its upstream siblings) establish.

## Approach

Add a "Code-review project criteria" section to `docs/llm-framework.md`, written as the **normative
contract** the completed seam provides (documentation of the delivered end-state, not a claim about
`origin/main`'s current code). Give each of the four aspects its own `####` subheading so the
contract is legible and testable aspect-by-aspect. The subheadings and their required marker tokens:

1. `#### Round A/B fan-in` — activated project criteria are fanned into the finder passes
   (`round_a`/`round_b`) and each finding carries its **logical criterion id** `project.<name>`.
2. `#### Effective routing in Pass 3` — the repository-scoped **effective routing** map governs the
   deterministic decision (threshold / blocking / nit-suppression).
3. `#### criteria eval resolution` — project code-review ids resolve and calibrate through the
   code-review arm of `criteria eval`.
4. `#### Configuration error` — an activated criterion with a missing prompt/eval spec raises a
   located **configuration error**.

**Rejected alternative.** A separate new doc file — the four-pass contract already lives in
`llm-framework.md`; splitting the code-review project-criterion contract elsewhere would fragment
the single authoritative reference.

## Scope

- `docs/llm-framework.md` — add the "Code-review project criteria" section (four `####` subheadings
  above).

(No `## Testing` section: mechanical docs change, no new testable behavior — the acceptance check
is a documentation-content assertion over the added section.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/llm-framework.md` gains a "Code-review project criteria" section containing **four
      distinct `####` subheadings** — one per contract aspect — so a single generic sentence cannot
      satisfy the check; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/python -c "from pathlib import Path; s=Path('docs/llm-framework.md').read_text(); heads=['#### Round A/B fan-in','#### Effective routing in Pass 3','#### criteria eval resolution','#### Configuration error']; missing=[h for h in heads if h not in s]; assert not missing, missing"`
      (exit 0 = all four subheadings present).
- [ ] Within that section body each aspect's marker token is present, so the subheadings are not
      empty stubs; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/python -c "from pathlib import Path; s=Path('docs/llm-framework.md').read_text(); assert 'Code-review project criteria' in s; sec=s.split('Code-review project criteria',1)[1][:5000]; markers=['round_a','logical criterion id','effective routing','criteria eval','configuration error']; missing=[m for m in markers if m not in sec]; assert not missing, missing"`
      (exit 0 = all five markers present in the section body).
